name: Support Cases - Salesforces to Jira - Creation & updates

code.name: updated_custom_object_webhook

input.field_list:
Id
CaseNumber
SuppliedName
Type
Status
Subject
Priority
Description
Product__c

extended_input_schema.hint: When starting the recipe for the first time, picks up Salesforce records from the specified date and time. Once the recipe has been run or tested, this value cannot be changed.
extended_input_schema.label: When first started, this recipe should pick up events from
extended_input_schema.name: since_offset

toggle_field.label: When first started, this recipe should pick up events from
toggle_field.hint: When starting the recipe for the first time, picks up Salesforce records from the specified date and time. Once the recipe has been run or tested, this value cannot be changed.
toggle_field.name: since

block.name: search_issues

extended_input_schema.hint:
Provide comma separated issue ids to get consistent results immediately after updating the issues. Accepts maximum 50 ids.
Note: Consistency is ensured only for specified issues.
extended_input_schema.label: Reconcile Issue IDs
extended_input_schema.name: reconcileIssues

block.comment: Check if the case is already existing in Jira by looking at the "summary" field of Jira issues that store the case nb of Salesforce (as foreign key between the 2 systems)

extended_input_schema.hint: Provide issue ID or key of parent issue, e.g. 10105 (issue ID) or ABC-123 (issue key).Use issue ID if your issue moves between projects.
extended_input_schema.label: Parent
extended_input_schema.name: parent

extended_input_schema.hint: Enter request participants separated by commas. If the values have comma in it, make sure to give it as array of string using formula mode. For example: ["A,B,C", "D,E,F"]

extended_input_schema.name: customfield_10034

extended_input_schema.name: customfield_10003

block.name: update_issue_status
block.comment: The status mapping from Salesforce to Jira is done by a Ruby ternary expression, with Jira worflow values defined on the project properties
If the status of the Salesforce task is updated during the creation process (different than "New"), it has to be updated in Jira to reflect it

input.transition_name: =_dp('{"pill_type":"output","provider":"salesforce","line":"680f3b81","path":["Status"]}')=="New" ? _dp('{"pill_type":"project_property","property_name":"Jira New Status"}') : _dp('{"pill_type":"output","provider":"salesforce","line":"680f3b81","path":["Status"]}')=="Working" ? _dp('{"pill_type":"project_property","property_name":"Jira InProgress Status"}') : _dp('{"pill_type":"output","provider":"salesforce","line":"680f3b81","path":["Status"]}')=="Closed" ? _dp('{"pill_type":"project_property","property_name":"Jira Close Status"}') : _dp('{"pill_type":"project_property","property_name":"Jira Escalated Status"}')

block.comment: This monitor block prevent recipe to stop when the update doesn't change the status in Jira (Info only)

If aJira task already exists with the Salesforce Case Nb as "summary", we will update this Issue in Jira, identify by the Jira issue Id. This update is done in 2 steps : the data of the issue, then the status
If no Issue Case exists with the Salesforce Case Nb as "summary"  a new Issue in Jira must be created

block.name: post_message_to_channel

input.text: Customer Case has been creater or updated in SalesForce
input.title: Salesforce link to the Case : #{_dp('{"pill_type":"output","provider":"salesforce","line":"680f3b81","path":["CaseNumber"]}')}

block.comment: The post message to Slack is done whatever the problems that may occurs during the Jira issue update or creation process. It is using only the information received by the trigger of the Recipe. This task is at the end of the Recipe, but it would be the same to place it just after the trigger task.

code.comment: This trigger retrieves new / updated Case in Salesforces - Single mode
